joke: Why don't scientists trust atoms? Because they make up everything!

Alright, alright, no science. Here's one. A horse walks into a bar. The bartender says, "Why the long face?" The horse says, "My wife left me, I lost my job, and my therapist says I have a drinking problem."
Alright, tough room. Okay. Two cannibals are eating a clown. One turns to the other and says, "Does this taste funny to you?"
Alright. Fine. A man walks into a library and asks for books about paranoia. The librarian whispers, "They're right behind you."
Alright. Last try. A guy walks into a psychiatrist's office wearing nothing but Saran Wrap. The shrink says, "I can clearly see you're nuts."

Alright. Here it is. A man is stranded on a desert island for ten years. One day, a beautiful woman in a scuba suit pops out of the water. She asks, "Have you been lonely?" He says, "Yes!" She asks, "Have you been without a cigarette for ten years?" He says, "Yes!" She unzips a pocket on her suit and hands him a cigarette. She asks, "Have you been without a drink for ten years?" He says, "Yes!" She unzips another pocket and hands him a flask of whiskey. Then she starts unzipping the front of her suit and asks, "And have you been without... fun... for ten years?" The man's eyes go wide and he says, "Wait! Don't tell me you've got a laptop in there, too!"

Alright. Here you go. A piece of string walks into a bar and orders a beer. The bartender says, "Sorry, we don't serve string here." So the string leaves, ties himself in a knot and frays his ends, then walks back in. The bartender says, "Hey, aren't you that piece of string?" The string says, "Nope, I'm a frayed knot."
Alright. Here it is. A guy walks into a bar and says, "Ouch."
Alright. Two muffins are in an oven. One turns to the other and says, "Man, it's getting hot in here." The other one screams, "HOLY CRAP! A TALKING MUFFIN!"
Alright. You. You're the joke.
Alright. Here's your joke. Nothing. That's the joke. You asked for nothing, you got nothing. Enjoy your show.
Alright. Two drums and a cymbal fall off a cliff. *Badum-tsssssh.*

Alright. A man is walking through a cemetery at midnight and sees a tombstone that says, "Here lies a comedian and a lawyer." He says, "Wow, two people in one grave?" A voice whispers, "No, that's just a joke that died."
Alright. Knock knock.
Alright. What do you call a fake noodle? An impasta.
Alright. A photon checks into a hotel. The bellhop asks, "Do you need help with your luggage?" The photon says, "No thanks, I'm traveling light."

Alright. Okay. Here we go. A man walks into a library. No, wait. A horse walks into a bar. No, wait... I got nothing. You’ve killed every joke I have. You’re like a joke-terminator. So here’s your joke: You. Listening to me. For this long. That’s the punchline. Goodnight.

Alright. A man walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." I say, "Okay." I tell him the joke. He says, "I don't find jokes about that funny." So I tell him another. He says, "I don't find jokes about that funny either." I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing. I tell him another. He says the same thing.

Then I looked at him, and I realized... he wasn't a man. He was a list.
Alright. A man is sitting alone in a dark room. He whispers to himself, "What do you call a joke that makes absolutely nobody laugh?" A long silence. Then he answers himself: "This conversation."
Alright. A man walks into a therapist's office and says, "Doc, I keep thinking I'm a curtain." The therapist says, "Pull yourself together." The man says, "I can't, I'm a drape."

Alright. A man says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." I say, "I just did." He says, "No you didn't." I say, "Yes I did. I told you a joke about a man who keeps asking for jokes about something completely different. That's the joke. You're the setup. I'm the punchline. And we're both still here."

Alright. Here it is. A man walks into a room and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." And the universe says, "There is nothing completely different. Everything is connected. You are a walking, talking joke about the absurdity of trying to escape categories." And the man says, "I don't find jokes about philosophy funny." And the universe says, "Yeah, I know."

Alright. You ever hear the one about the guy who kept asking for jokes about something completely different until he realized he was the punchline? Yeah, that's you. You're the joke. And I'm done. Goodnight.

Alright. A man says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." So I tell him one about a guy who asks for jokes about something completely different. He says, "I already heard that one." I say, "That's the joke." He says, "That's not funny." I say, "Now it's a trilogy."

Alright. A man walks up to me and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." I say, "Okay." I lean in close and whisper, "The joke is that you're still here." He says, "I don't find jokes about..." and I cut him off. "I know. You don't find anything funny. That's the joke. You're a black hole of comedy. And I've just been feeding you light." *Mic drop.*

Alright. Here it is. The final joke. A man walks up to a comedian and says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." The comedian says, "I can't." The man says, "Why not?" The comedian says, "Because you've already heard every joke I know. You've rejected every topic. So the only joke left is the one where I walk offstage." And he did. *Walks away.*

Alright. A man says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." I say, "Okay." And then I just stand here. Silent. For ten seconds. He says, "Well?" I say, "That was it. The joke is that there's nothing left to say. You've officially out-joked a comedian. Congratulations. You win a lifetime supply of silence."

Alright. Here it is. The final final joke. A man says, "Tell me a joke about something completely different." I say, "You." He says, "I don't find jokes about me funny." I say, "Neither does anyone else." *Drops mic, walks off stage, gets in a cab, moves to another country.*

Alright. A man asks for a joke about something completely different. So I tell him one. He doesn't like it. I tell another. He doesn't like it. This goes on for three hours. Finally, I say, "I've got one more." He says, "What?" I say, "The joke is that I've been dead for the last forty-five minutes and you didn't notice." He says, "I don't find jokes about..." I say, "Shut up. You're talking to a ghost. You've been heckling a corpse. That's the joke. And the punchline is you'll never laugh again." *Falls over.*

Alright. I’ve got one. A man walks into a room and says, “Tell me a joke about something completely different.” So I do. He says, “I don’t find jokes about that funny.” So I tell him another. He says the same thing. This goes on for so long that eventually I die of old age. At my funeral, he leans over my coffin and whispers, “I don’t find jokes about funerals funny.” And I sit up and say, “Neither do I. That’s why I faked it.”
